feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Can't add image descriptions to images in community blog posts anymore
entry.content:
Hey, 

I wasn't sure which category to put this in, let me know if there's another place to ask this question. 

I'm trying to write a block post here in the SAP community but I can't click in the text field below an image in the image upload section anymore. I checked it in a new blog post, in the one I've been working on for the past days and older already released ones. 


When I'm opening older blogpost, the description is still there, but I can't edit it (because I can't click in the field either).

Any suggestions on how to proceed or what the issue could be? 

 

Thanks for your help and cheers,
jadga

 

p.s.: I tried the Chrome and Firefox browsers and have logged in and out again several times, doesn't change anything.

entry.title: Join validation error converting universe from Oracle to SQL Server
entry.content: We are in the process of converting our databases and universes from Oracle to SQL Server as required by a vendor.  When I try to validate some of the joins in IDT, I get an arithmetic overflow error that appears to be caused by the validation query using a count(*) instead of a count_big(*).  Is there any workaround or setting I can use to get around this error?

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud Trial Instance Fails Repeatedly During Creation
entry.content:
I am using an SAP BTP Trial account and attempting to create an SAP HANA Cloud (hana-free) instance.

The instance repeatedly fails during creation or becomes unavailable after initial setup. I have retried multiple times, verified quotas, and followed all standard setup steps, but the issue persists.

Environment details:
- Account type: SAP BTP Trial
- Service: SAP HANA Cloud (hana-free)
- Subaccount: trial
- Issue: Instance creation/startup failure

This is blocking an academic capstone project that depends on SAP HANA Cloud.

Has anyone faced this issue recently on the trial landscape, or is there a way to reset the HANA Cloud service for trial users?

entry.title: EWM: How to determine HU/Packing status before Set Ready for Shipment to avoid printing duplicate
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are working on an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud EWM scenario with Goods Issue (GI) label printing and are facing a challenge related to duplicate label generation.

Current Process

  • GI labels are printed during the packing process in EWM, triggered by the Warehouse Task Confirmed event.
  • During packing:
    • An internal Warehouse Task (WT) is created
    • GI label is printed based on the WT number
    • A Handling Unit (HU) is created as part of the packing process

      New Requirement

      • Print the GI label at the Set Ready for Shipment stage using the Outbound Delivery Change event.
      • This should happen only for deliveries that are not packed into an HU.

        Issue

        • When the delivery is set to Ready for Shipment, GI labels are also getting printed for deliveries that are already packed in HU, resulting in duplicate label printing.

          Analysis Done

          • We explored checking the packing status (HU existence) using:
          • I_OutboundDelivery 
          • I_OutboundDeliveryItem 
          • However, since packing and label printing are executed within EWM, these CDS views are not updated in real time during the packing process.
          • We also could not find any EWM-specific CDS views that reliably expose real-time packing or HU status.

            Question
            Is there a recommended or alternative approach in EWM to reliably determine whether:

            • Packing has already been completed, or
            • An HU already exists for the delivery
              before triggering GI label printing at the Set Ready for Shipment stage, so that duplicate printing can be avoided?

              Any guidance on best practices, EWM events, APIs, CDS views, or extensibility options would be highly appreciated.

              Thanks in advance for your support

              •

entry.title: Creating cheque batches
entry.content:
Good morning,

I've recently joined the group as of yesterday. I have a question regarding cheque batches. In my old system, we had the ability to cut cheques via a cheque batch with several different vendors. If that is possible, how is it done in the system?

Thanks

Jennifer

entry.title: C_IEE2E Practical Exam Under Review → Attempt Closed & Recredited – Has Anyone Experienced This?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to check if anyone else has experienced a similar situation with the new system-based practical certification exams, particularly C_IEE2E – SAP Certified – Implementation Consultant – End-to-End Business Processes for SAP Business Suite.

My situation:

  • I completed the C_IEE2E practical exam on 25 January 2026.

  • The exam status remained “Under Review” for several days (despite results usually being available shortly after completion).

  • I contacted SAP Certification Support.

  • I later received a response stating that:

    • They cannot provide detailed information on why the attempt was unsuccessful.

    • The certification is currently in a pilot phase, and detailed score reports are not yet available.

    • My attempt has been closed and recredited.

While I appreciate the recredit, I am now a bit unsure about the next best step.

My questions to the community and SAP team:

  1. Has anyone else faced a similar situation with C_IEE2E or other system-based practical exams?

  2. If your attempt was recredited, did you:

    • Retake the same exam immediately?

    • Wait for process improvements or clearer guidance?

  3. Are there known stability or evaluation issues with the pilot practical exams that candidates should be aware of?

  4. Would you recommend proceeding with a retake now, or waiting until the enhanced score reporting process is fully available?

Additionally, I am planning to take the following certifications soon:

  • SAP Certified – Implementation Consultant – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Financial Accounting

  • SAP Certified – Implementation Consultant – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Management Accounting

Given this experience, I would really value advice on whether it’s better to:

  • Continue with these certifications now, or

  • Pause until the practical exam processes are more mature.

I’m sharing this to learn from others’ experiences and to get guidance from SAP experts or community members who may have gone through something similar.

Thank you in advance for your insights.

Kind regards,

entry.title: Need SAP PUblic Cloud system access
entry.content: Need help to get free acccess of Grow with SAP Public Cloud by using S - User ID

entry.title: Not Getting Email Notifications for Comments Posted on my Blog Posts
entry.content:
Hello Community Admin Team,

Since some time, it is noted that I am not getting Emails for the Comments posted on my Blog Post. This is so when is no notification settings change (Email for Comments on my subscribed items still remains set) and when I am already subscribed to my post.

This was observed by my other colleague also. Tried changing the settings but no luck. Example post - Managing Invoices with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

Kindly check on this. Could be problem with others too. Missing to reply on important comments.

 - Abhijit

SAP Community

entry.title: Depreciation Posted Monthly Despite Planned Useful Life of 1 Period
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community, 

We are facing an issue with asset depreciation and would appreciate some guidance.

We have an asset that was created in March, with the capitalization date 31.03.2025. In transaction AS03, under Depreciation Area 01 (HB1), the following settings are maintained:

  • Depreciation Key: "Linear over remaining useful life, pro rata, down to zero"

  • Planned useful life in periods: 1

  • Depreciation calculation start date: 01.01.2025

Despite these settings, depreciation is being posted every month, which is not what we expect given the planned useful life of 1 period.

We initially tried changing the depreciation key to "100% depreciation" to see if this would resolve the issue — could this be a valid approach, or might it cause other inconsistencies?

Additionally, we are using transaction AFAR (Recalculate Depreciation):

  • How should AFAR be used correctly in this scenario?

  • Is it required after changing the depreciation key or useful life settings to ensure correct depreciation posting?

Any insights into the root cause or best practice for handling this would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: When flagging the pricing condition for deletion a second condition also marked for deltion.
entry.content:
Hello everyone, We have an issue when flagging a condition record for deletion. When deleting a condition which has the infinite date as validity end, another condition record with the same price is also marked for deletion.

In the above screenshot, we are deleting the condition valid from 01.05.2028 to 31.12.9999 and system also flags the condition 01.01.2026 to 01.31.2026 for deletion. Both these condition has the same amount as price. The condition type is a copy of PR00 condition type. 

The only connection i can think off is, originally the condition ending on 31.12.9999 had the starting date as 01.01.2026 and it was moved to 01.05.2026 as the other conditions were added. 

Appreciate your help

SKR

entry.title: Suggestion for S4 Master Data Governance
entry.content:
I want to learn and do certification in S4 Master Data Governance, please suggest course(s) as a beginner to expert for same on SAP Learning Hub. I see following courses there, but I guess this is not right course to start with.

Exploring the SAP Master Data Governance Deployments

entry.title: Hi everyone, please help with the solution as per below explained scenario.
entry.content:
Scenario- We are creating the WBS structure with budget allocation to child WBS element and Parent WBS does not hold any budget. We marked the parent WBS as Grouping WBS with value-1, and assigned all child WBS to it. Manually PR/ PO creation is being done with Child WBS. On GRN, inventory is available in child WBS.

To produce finished goods, production order for project (CO10) is created with assignment of parent WBS. To allow consumption of inventory from child WBS, components are assigned with respective inventory WBS.

Goods issue, order confirmation and finished goods receipt is done. But the value gets assigned to parent WBS and the value/ cost equivalent to inventory that is consumed again becomes available to child WBS.

I want to execute the PMMO function for this, so that pegging will establish the relationship and distribution will do the CO posting to respective child WBS (equivalent to consumption cost).

This is not possible with PS alone.

But is it possible to achieve with PMMO & PS? If yes, how to proceed? what are excess WBS, breakpoint WBS in pegging. 

outcome require: actual value shall be assigned to child WBS equivalent to value of inventory consumption, after distribution run

entry.title: SAC BW Standard Queries
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I try to fetch the standard queries in BW platform so that i got the queries which is shown in the snapshot.
But the problem is there around 470 queries are there out of those queries how to find out exact query which is have the sales register data or PS module data and finance data etc....

Please help me with the queries, I need to create a report for SAP PS module it's an emergency 
SO please me in this.


Regards,
Manikanta.

entry.title: Unable to use brand 72 font on SharePoint
entry.content: Hello, I have a doubt: when we create or open a document in SharePoint, the 72 font is not available. If we open the same file on the app (on a local computer instead of a browser), the font will be available. Are there any steps missing, or is it normal? Your help and suggestions would be appreciated.

entry.title: Need Guidance: MRP-Area Based Scheduling for Subcontracting Components (REM + Stock Determination)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a requirement to generate different schedule lines for the same component based on the parent material’s PIR source (i.e., FG/parent material). The scenario spans buy materials, subcontracting materials, and components in subcontracting BOMs.

Requirement

When a component is common across multiple FGs, its source of supply should be selected based on the originating FG:

  • Example: Component COMP1 exists in FG1 and FG2.
    • If the requirement originates from FG1, MRP should select SCH1 (Scheduling Agreement 1).
    • If it originates from FG2, MRP should select SCH2 (Scheduling Agreement 2).

Context: We are in a REM scenario. Components are consumed via stock determination rule/group; after MRP, created planned orders are consumed upon MFBF posting.

Approach Adopted

To drive different schedule lines for the same component, we proposed:

  • MRP execution by MRP Area (MRP Area = Storage Location)
  • Maintain two different scheduling agreements in the source list, separated by MRP Area.

Result:
When we maintain the storage location in the BOM, the requirement flows to the respective MRP Area, and we can generate different schedule lines for the same component. This works fine for:

  • Normal buy materials, and
  • Subcontracting materials (header)

Issues Faced

1) Subcontracting Component Requirements Ignoring MRP Areas

For components of a subcontracting material, the requirement is always pushed to the plant level, even when MRP Areas are maintained. Consequently, only one schedule line is picked from the source list.

  • Tried BADIs MD_EXT_SUP and MD_MODIFY_SOURCE, but they don’t help because the BAdIs receive the aggregated requirement without a clear reference to the originating FG/requirement source.

Ask: Guidance or pointers on how to ensure subcontracting components respect MRP Area–based sourcing so that MRP picks different schedule lines based on the originating FG.

2) Stock Determination Disrupted by Storage Location in BOM

Maintaining the storage location in the BOM (for COMP1) disrupts our stock determination rule—stock is then consumed only from the BOM’s fixed storage location (standard SAP behavior).

Mitigation Attempt: Two Production Versions

  • PV1 / BOM1: For MFBF posting (no storage location in BOM) → stock determination works correctly.
  • PV2 / BOM2: Identical to BOM1 but with storage location maintained for MRP Area–based MRP → ensures different schedule lines during MRP.

Problem:
MRP creates planned orders with PV2, but MFBF postings are executed with PV1. As a result, planned orders are not consumed and remain open.

Ask: How should we proceed?

  • Option 1 (Single BOM):
    • Pros: MRP creates different schedule lines; planned orders are consumed during MFBF
    • Con: Stock determination rule is disrupted
  • Option 2 (Two BOMs / PVs):
    • Pros: MFBF posting is correct; different schedule lines created correctly
    • Con: Planned orders remain unconsumed

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: What is the SAP field in material master for the connection with SAF-T?
entry.content:
Hello.

Which module in SAP MM or SD do you use to link a field from the material master data to a field from the SAF-T material tables?

What is the SAP field in material master for the connection with SAF-T?

Regards

P.Kirilov

entry.title: SAP FICO Certification
entry.content:
Hi All (SAP learners and Professionals),

I have started preparing for SAP FI/CO modules and would like the community members to guide me on how to prepare for it in the best way so that I can increase my chances to clear the certifications. Also I am hearing that MCQs type exam pattern is getting changed in 2026 so any information you have please share it with me.

Regards,

Vipin Gupta (SAP Enthusiast)

entry.title: Historical Revision level on POs
entry.content: Which transaction can I use to see the last revision level of a material placed in a PO (I want to see this in a massive way)

entry.title: Hana SQL Command Network Protocol Deprecation/Sunset
entry.content: I remember reading somewhere that the Hana SQL Command Network Protocol might be getting shut down in the next few years, but I was unable to find that information when I went looking for it. As the new products move away from using that protocol and some of the official drivers on GitHub are no longer being supported (Python), does anyone know of any plans to shut down that protocol for future versions of the Hana product? If so, is there a definitive timeline for that?

entry.title: C_TS4FI_2601 SAP Certified - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Financial Accounting
entry.content: The presentation of the exam is a system-based evaluation. Last week I took the exam C_TS4FI_2601 SAP Certified - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Financial Accounting, and I failed it. Do you know if the exam is the same for the next attempt or does it change? Someone who has taken it, could you please share your experience with me? Thank you.
